plan_title: feat: SEO Orchestrator Skill — Phase 1 MVP
scope: Single-user internal automation tool for WordPress.com site (yololab.net, SITE_ID: 133512998). Unifies 4 existing SEO optimization scripts into conversational skill. No user-facing auth, no payments, no new endpoints.

findings.title: Credentials stored in plaintext .env.local file in working directory
findings.why_it_matters: WordPress.com authentication password visible on disk (WORDPRESS_PASSWORD: WVOD RQw4 62NK 5BPl 1lTE lryv in .env.local, line 4). Shared auth chain (env vars, .env, .mcp.json) reads from disk with no encryption. If developer's machine compromised or repo accidentally committed, credentials are exposed.
evidence: .env.local contains WORDPRESS_PASSWORD=WVOD RQw4 62NK 5BPl 1lTE lryv (verified)
image-alt-text-optimizer.js lines 100-127 read .env file with regex match: fs.readFileSync(envPath) + envContent.match(/(?:WP_APP_USER|WP_USERNAME)=(.*)/)
Plan Unit 1 (Shared Utilities, line 152) specifies auth discovery chain but does not address disk-based credential protection
Plan notes auth fallback to .mcp.json (line 86) which itself may contain bearer tokens
findings.mitigation_required: Add credential rotation policy docs; require environment variable-only auth for production (strip .env fallback); document that .env.local must be in .gitignore; consider OS keyring integration for interactive use.

findings.title: Credential exposure in logs and error messages
findings.section: Data Exposure - Logs and Error Handling
findings.why_it_matters: Scripts use console.log for debug output (image-alt-text-optimizer.js line 163: `log()` function). If auth fails or API errors occur, credentials in header objects may leak to stderr/logs. Plan Unit 5 mentions 'structured reports' but does not specify log redaction.
evidence: image-alt-text-optimizer.js defines log() at line 163 using console.log without sanitization
Error handling via retries (apiGet/apiPost) likely logs fetch failures with headers intact
Plan Unit 5 (line 328-340) defines report structure but no mention of log redaction for secrets
Bash tool output captured by Claude Code harness — logs visible in session
findings.mitigation_required: Create redaction utility in seo-shared.js (Unit 1) that masks Authorization headers, bearer tokens, and basic auth credentials before logging. Apply to all fetch error logs and debug output.

findings.title: Global lock file has 8-hour TTL but incomplete stale-lock recovery
findings.why_it_matters: Plan specifies global lock (`seo-orchestrator.lock`) with 8-hour TTL (line 78, line 157). Edge case (line 172): 'Lock file exists but is stale (>TTL), acquires anyway.' If Claude session crashes mid-batch, lock persists for 8 hours, blocking legitimate resume. Attacker could intentionally lock the system for extended period if lock mechanism is exploitable.
evidence: Plan line 78: 'Global lock: single seo-orchestrator.lock file with 8-hour TTL'
Plan line 172: Edge case for stale lock — 'Lock file exists but is stale (>TTL), acquires anyway'
Plan line 409: Risk noted — 'Long batches (6-8h) span multiple Claude sessions', mitigation is orchestrator state file + lock
image-alt-text-optimizer.js lines 71, 215-227: existing lock implementation uses timestamp, but no documented recovery procedure for orphaned locks
findings.mitigation_required: Document lock recovery workflow: UUID-based locks with process metadata; `npm run seo:unlock` command with password/token to force release; logs all lock acquisitions/releases. Reduce TTL from 8h to 4h for better recovery window.

findings.title: Backup files store full post content; disk space and cleanup not addressed
findings.why_it_matters: Plan Unit 3 (line 244) and Unit 4 (line 289) require backup of full `post.content` before writes. For internal-links module, plan acknowledges backup will be '50-100MB for full site' but provides no cleanup policy. Risk: unbounded disk usage, unencrypted backups persist on disk.
evidence: Plan Unit 3 line 244: 'Before writing meta fields, fetch current values... and save to backup JSON'
Plan Unit 4 line 289: 'Before modifying post.content, save to seo-optimization-output/internal-links-backup.json. This will be large (~50-100MB for full site)'

Plan line 406: Risk identified: 'Large backup files for internal-links (~50-100MB)' with mitigation 'Monitor disk usage; add cleanup for backups >30 days old' — but this is deferred, not implemented in Phase 1
Plan line 304: Test scenario expects 'Large backup file (~50MB) reads/writes correctly' but no automated cleanup
findings.mitigation_required: Unit 1 (Shared Utilities, line 138): add backup helper function with parameterized retention policy (default 7 days). Unit 5 (Orchestrator): log backup size after each module; warn user if >100MB. Add `npm run seo:cleanup-backups` command.

findings.title: Orchestrator state file tracks batch progress but unencrypted on disk
findings.why_it_matters: Plan Unit 5 (line 340) defines `seo-orchestrator-state.json` to persist active module, batch progress, user intent across sessions. File stored unencrypted in `seo-optimization-output/`. Attacker or malicious process on same machine could read batch progress, article IDs, and timing information.
evidence: Plan line 340: 'Orchestrator state (seo-orchestrator-state.json): { activeModule, batchId, startTime, articlesProcessed, articlesRemaining, userIntent, lastUpdated }'
Plan line 341: 'Quick audit cache (seo-optimization-output/audit-cache.json)': same directory, same plaintext storage
Plan line 327: State stored in working directory without access control specification
findings.mitigation_required: Clarify: state file is metadata only (no secrets), but should have mode 0600 (user-only read/write) on Unix. Document that seo-optimization-output/ directory is single-user workspace, not shared. Consider moving state to OS-specific cache directory (.claude/ on Linux, ~/Library/ on macOS, %APPDATA% on Windows).

findings.title: No rate-limit sharing across concurrent module invocations within orchestrator state
findings.why_it_matters: Plan specifies 'Module execution is sequential (shared API rate limit), not parallel' (line 343), but each module (image-alt, meta-tags, schema, internal-links) imports seo-shared.js with independent rate limit config (batchSize: 5, delayMs: 2000, line 154). If modules are invoked sequentially without global rate limiter coordination, requests may still exceed WordPress.com's actual per-site limits.
evidence: Plan line 343: 'Module execution is sequential (shared API rate limit), not parallel'
Plan line 154: Rate limit config as parameter: '{ batchSize, delayMs, retries, backoffMs }'
Plan line 408: Risk: 'WordPress.com API rate limits change' with mitigation 'Rate limit config is parameterized' — but no shared global rate limiter mentioned
Unit 1 does not specify a global rate limiter object shared across all modules
findings.mitigation_required: Unit 1 (Shared Utilities): add global rate-limit tracker (in-memory queue or file-based checkpoint) that coordinates all module API calls. Each module logs rate-limit headers from responses. If 429 detected, back off globally and notify orchestrator to pause.

findings.title: REST API writes bypass wpcom-mcp safety protocol — no per-write confirmation
findings.why_it_matters: Plan explicitly states (line 72): 'wpcom-mcp requires per-write user confirmation, making batch operations (2,728 articles) impractical. MCP can be used for discovery (listing sites, checking capabilities) but all read/write operations go through direct REST API.' This is an architectural decision to bypass MCP's safety layer. While justified by UX, it means no framework-level safeguard for destructive operations.
evidence: Plan line 72: Explicit decision documented — 'REST API for all writes, not wpcom-mcp'
Plan line 38: 'No wpcom-mcp for writes — direct REST API only (per-write confirmation conflict)'
Plan Unit 5 line 335: Skill shows confirmation 'before batch writes: article count, estimated time, cost estimate, 3 sample changes, backup status'
Orchestrator does client-side confirmation (skill prompt) but this is not a technical guarantee
findings.mitigation_required: Unit 5 (Orchestrator Skill): require explicit opt-in flag (e.g., `--confirm batch`) for batch operations >100 articles. Document that confirmation happens in Claude UI (not wpcom-mcp), and confirm it's an informed decision by the *user* via the `/seo` conversation. Add warning to skill prompt about destructive operations.

findings.title: No auth verification before module execution starts
findings.why_it_matters: Plan Unit 1 (line 158) specifies 'Auth health check: verifyAuth(config) → { ok, method, apiBase, error }' but Plan Unit 3 (line 246) describes 'Add auth verification: Before first write, verify that WPCOM_TOKEN can write to _yoast_wpseo_* fields' as a *per-module* check. If first auth discovery fails silently, orchestrator may proceed with stale/invalid credentials.
evidence: Plan Unit 1 line 158: verifyAuth() specified for Unit 1
Plan Unit 3 line 246: 'verify that WPCOM_TOKEN can write to _yoast_wpseo_* fields. If Yoast fields are rejected, surface error immediately instead of failing silently per-article'
Plan Unit 1 test scenarios (line 174): 'Error path: verifyAuth with no credentials returns descriptive error'
Plan Unit 5 does not document orchestrator-level auth check before module dispatch
findings.mitigation_required: Unit 5 (Orchestrator): Call verifyAuth() from Unit 1 before entering conversational mode. If auth fails, exit with clear error. Document that auth verification includes write capability test (e.g., test PATCH to a sample post meta field).

findings.title: Backup file encryption not specified; sensitive content (post body, drafts) at risk
findings.section: Data Exposure - Sensitive Content in Backups
findings.why_it_matters: Plan acknowledges (line 84) 'Backup files contain full post content (potentially drafts, unpublished).' Draft posts may contain sensitive notes, internal links, or unreleased announcements. Backup files stored unencrypted in seo-optimization-output/ accessible to any process on developer's machine.
evidence: Plan line 84 (system context): 'Backup files contain full post content (potentially drafts, unpublished)'
Plan Unit 4 line 289: Backup includes 'originalContent' for rollback — full Gutenberg block markup stored
Plan line 410: Risk noted — 'Large backup files for internal-links (~50-100MB)' but no encryption mitigation listed
image-alt-text-optimizer.js line 703: backup loaded via loadJSON() with no encryption
findings.mitigation_required: Document that backups should be treated as sensitive and stored with restricted file permissions (0600). Consider adding optional encryption via seo-shared.js backup helper. If not encrypting, document backup directory access controls in CLAUDE.md.

findings.title: Module wrapper export signatures and error contract not fully specified
findings.why_it_matters: Plan Unit 1-4 specify module exports (e.g., line 199: 'phaseScan(config, options)', line 242: 'generateMetaOptimization(article)') but exact function signatures, error handling contracts, and exception types are deferred: 'Deferred to Implementation' (line 89). Orchestrator (Unit 5) must call these modules but has no guaranteed interface. If module errors are not standardized, orchestrator error handling will be fragile.
evidence: Plan line 89: 'Exact function signatures for module wrapper exports — depends on seeing how cleanly globals can be replaced with parameters'
Plan Unit 2 line 199-202: Phase functions specified but return types/errors undefined
Plan Unit 5 line 337: Skill 'Execute by calling module scripts via Bash tool' — no documented error handling protocol
Test scenarios (e.g., line 209-213) describe happy paths and edge cases but not error object schema
findings.mitigation_required: Before implementation: define module interface contract (JSON schema or TypeScript interfaces). Each exported function must return { ok, data?, error?, stats, backupPath }. Document that errors are logged to stderr, exit code >0 signals failure. Orchestrator skill must parse exit code and error object to decide rollback.

findings.title: WPCOM_TOKEN env var unrotated; no mechanism for token refresh or revocation
findings.why_it_matters: Plan unifies on WPCOM_TOKEN as canonical env var (line 86) and specifies no rotation policy. If WPCOM_TOKEN is compromised, attacker has persistent write access to yololab.net until manually revoked. Plan does not address token lifetime, rotation schedules, or detection of unauthorized use.
evidence: Plan line 86: 'Auth standardization: Unify on WPCOM_TOKEN as the canonical env var'
Plan Unit 1 line 152: Auth discovery chain prioritizes WPCOM_TOKEN but no rotation documented
Plan risks & dependencies (line 406): No mention of token compromise, rotation, or audit trails
.mcp.json line 19: wpcom-mcp URL does not include token versioning or rotation endpoint
findings.mitigation_required: Document in CLAUDE.md: WPCOM_TOKEN should be application password or API token with short TTL (if WordPress.com supports). Recommend quarterly rotation. Add optional `--verify-auth` flag to orchestrator to test token validity without writes. Consider logging API call counts to detect unauthorized access.

findings.title: Audit cache may become stale; no expiration enforcement in orchestrator
findings.why_it_matters: Plan specifies audit cache (`seo-optimization-output/audit-cache.json`) with suggested expiration (7 days) but orchestrator only 'offers' fresh scan if cache is stale (Plan Unit 5, line 354). If user dismisses fresh scan, orchestrator proceeds with 30-day-old coverage data, making recommendations on incorrect assumptions.
evidence: Plan line 341: 'Quick audit cache (seo-optimization-output/audit-cache.json): { timestamp, modules: {...} }'
Plan line 76: 'Use cached scan results (<7 days old) when available; otherwise sample 100 articles and extrapolate'
Plan Unit 5 line 354: 'Edge case: Cached audit is >7 days old — triggers fresh sample scan'
Plan Unit 5 line 355: 'Verification: Cross-session resume works' — no test for cache expiration enforcement
findings.mitigation_required: Unit 5 (Orchestrator): enforce hard cache expiration (>7 days = cache invalid, fresh scan required). Document in skill prompt that stale data may lead to suboptimal recommendations. Add `--force-refresh` flag to skip cache.

findings.title: Module interdependencies not fully mapped; potential cascading failures
findings.why_it_matters: Plan specifies 'No hard dependencies between modules' (line 29) and lists dependencies as 'Unit 1 (shared utilities)' for Units 2-4 and 'Units 1-4' for Unit 5 (line 322). However, if Unit 1 (shared utilities) exports a buggy retry function, all 4 modules fail simultaneously. Plan does not document fallback strategies or graceful degradation if one module is unavailable.
evidence: Plan line 29: 'R15. No hard dependencies between modules'
Plan Unit 1 line 144: 'Dependencies: None — this is the foundation'
Plan Unit 5 line 322: 'Dependencies: Units 1-4 (all modules ready)'
Plan Unit 5 line 337-340: Skill executes modules sequentially but no documented fallback if one module fails
findings.mitigation_required: Unit 5 (Orchestrator): if a module fails (non-zero exit), log error and offer user choice: (a) skip module and continue with next, (b) retry module, (c) abort batch. Document that orchestrator state tracks which modules have run, allowing resume to skip completed modules.

findings.title: No audit logging for batch operations; compliance and forensics risk
findings.why_it_matters: Plan specifies structured reports per module (Unit 5, line 340) but does not mandate audit logging: who ran what, when, on which articles, with what results. If unauthorized changes occur, no trace to detect compromise. Single-user tool, but no forensic trail for reconciliation.
evidence: Plan Unit 5 line 340: 'Orchestrator state (seo-orchestrator-state.json): { activeModule, batchId, startTime, ... }'
Plan line 19: 'Structured summary report after execution' (R19) — defined as user-facing report, not audit log
Plan Unit 5 test (line 351-356): no test for audit trail or forensic logging
Risks & Dependencies (line 406): no risk identified for unlogged changes
findings.mitigation_required: Unit 1 (Shared Utilities): add logging utility that writes immutable audit log to seo-optimization-output/audit-log.jsonl (one JSON per line). Each batch operation logs: timestamp, module, articles affected, before/after diffs (sample), auth method used. Keep audit log for 90 days.

residual_risks.finding: Credential compromise (WPCOM_TOKEN stolen via disk or logs)
residual_risks.impact: High (full write access to yololab.net; 2,728+ articles exposed)
suggested_mitigations: Enforce env-var-only auth in production; document .env.local as dev-only fallback
Add credential redaction utility to log handler
Implement WPCOM_TOKEN rotation policy (quarterly minimum)
Monitor API call patterns for anomalies (e.g., bulk deletes)

residual_risks.finding: Unbounded backup disk usage; old backups not cleaned up
residual_risks.impact: Medium (disk full blocks batch operations; PII from drafts may persist)
suggested_mitigations: Implement backup retention policy (7 days) in Unit 1
Add `seo:cleanup-backups` npm script
Log backup size warnings during orchestrator execution
Document seo-optimization-output/ as non-persistent (may be wiped)

residual_risks.finding: Module interface contract not standardized; orchestrator may crash on module errors
residual_risks.impact: High (batch operation aborts; partial state committed; difficult recovery)
suggested_mitigations: Define module interface (function signatures, error schema) before any implementation
Unit 5: parse module exit codes and stderr to detect errors
Add state rollback tests (e.g., start→fail→resume→retry)

residual_risks.finding: Stale audit cache used to make recommendations; outdated data drives decisions
residual_risks.impact: Medium (suboptimal optimization; missed opportunities; wasted API calls)
suggested_mitigations: Unit 5: enforce cache expiration (>7 days = invalid)
Log cache age in orchestrator output
Document data quality assumptions in skill prompt

residual_risks.finding: Global lock file orphaned if Claude crashes; 8-hour recovery window
residual_risks.probability: Low (Claude stable) but impact is High (system unusable for hours)
residual_risks.impact: High (batch operations blocked; no automated recovery for 8h)
suggested_mitigations: Reduce lock TTL to 4 hours
Document UUID-based lock recovery procedure
Add `seo:unlock --password <token>` command
Log all lock acquisitions with process metadata

residual_risks.finding: Draft posts included in backups; sensitive unpublished content stored unencrypted
residual_risks.probability: High (by design; backups capture full post content)
residual_risks.impact: Medium (PII, unreleased announcements exposed if machine compromised)
suggested_mitigations: Set backup file permissions to 0600 (user-only)
Document backup directory as sensitive; include in .gitignore
Consider optional encryption via Unit 1 backup helper
Add backup anonymization option for shared dev environments

deferred_questions.question: What is the exact function signature contract for module exports (e.g., phaseScan, generateMetaOptimization)?
deferred_questions.suggested_resolution: Define JSON schema or TypeScript interfaces for all exported functions before Unit 2 implementation. Include: input types, return types, error types, side effects (file writes, API calls).

deferred_questions.question: Does WordPress.com support writing to _yoast_wpseo_* custom post meta fields on the active plan for yololab.net?
deferred_questions.suggested_resolution: Before Unit 3 implementation: run test against yololab.net to confirm Yoast fields are writable via WPCOM_TOKEN. Document the capability or fallback strategy if unsupported.

deferred_questions.question: What is the intended behavior when a module fails mid-batch? Rollback all, skip failed articles, or abort?
deferred_questions.suggested_resolution: Unit 5 design phase: specify error recovery policy. Options: (a) per-module rollback on fail, (b) continue with remaining modules, (c) offer user choice. Update orchestrator state schema to track partial failures.

deferred_questions.question: Should audit logs be included in backup cleanup, or retained indefinitely for forensics?
deferred_questions.suggested_resolution: Document audit log retention policy in CLAUDE.md. Recommended: keep logs for 90 days. If compliance required, extend to 1 year and consider separate audit log directory.

deferred_questions.question: What minimum auth permission test should verifyAuth() perform? Read-only, or test write capability?
deferred_questions.suggested_resolution: Unit 1: verifyAuth() should test write capability by attempting a no-op update (e.g., PATCH post meta with same value). This catches permission issues before batch starts.

blockers_for_implementation: Unit 1 must define module interface contract before Unit 2-4 can implement wrappers
Credential security policy must be documented before env var standardization (Unit 1)
Yoast SEO field write support must be verified on yololab.net before Unit 3 implementation
